content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 29, 2009


Michael B. Montgomery
1933 Mill Road
South Pasadena, CA 91030

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-158

Dear Mr. Montgomery:

This letter is in response to your request for advice dated June 8, 2009 regarding the duties of the City of South Pasadena under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note, pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) (copy enclosed), unless you are the authorized representative of the person whose duties you request advice about, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. Moreover, your question involves conduct that has already occurred, and the Commission does not provide advice on past conduct. (See, Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(b)(8)A&(C).)

In addition, your question primarily concerns laws that are currently outside the jurisdiction of Commission regulations. While the Act does require committees to report receipt of contributions from state or local government agencies, and requires that if a state or local government agency has enough campaign activity to qualify as a committee, the state or local government agency itself will be required to file campaign statements,² Commission regulations do not currently prohibit the expenditure. Under many circumstances, such activities may be illegal under other provisions of the law. (See, Penal Code, § 424; Gov. Code, §§ 8314 and 54964.)

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² See Regulation 18420; Winder Advice Letter, No. I-08-021, copy enclosed. If you believe there has been a violation of the Act and desire to file a complaint, visit the Commission's website at the following link: www.fppc.ca.gov.

Michael B. Montgomery
Page-2

Of some relevance, however, are proposed Commission Regulation 18901.1 and
proposed amendments to Regulation 18420.1 (copies enclosed). These regulations were
considered by the Commission at its June 2009 Commission Meeting and put over for further
consideration at its September 2009 meeting. We would welcome any comments you may have
on these proposals.

If you have other questions on this request for advice, please contact me. If you have
comments on the proposed regulations, you may contact Commission Counsel Brian Lau. We
both can be reached at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

Enclosures
